title: polygala vulgaris subsp. caliptera
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. caliptera, also known as common polygala or running milkwort, is a type of flowering plant in the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, to treat a range of health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Polygala vulgaris subsp. caliptera include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the roots and leaves of the plant and then infusing them in hot water or a solvent.
